item.dc:creator: KawaiiInari

item.title: Tacos for Lunch?
item.description: Birria de Cordero Recipe by Chef Nico Perez 1 lamb shank 3 onions 3 tomatoes 5 garlic cloves 4 guajillo peppers deveined/deseeded 4 ancho peppers deveined/deseeded 3 cups of water to hydrate the peppers 1.5 litres vegetable stock 2 tablespoons oregano 5 bay leaves 1 cinnamon stick - Consommé - Grill tomatoes, onions, and garlic until soft and caramelised. Hydrate the peppers in the water. Blend all these together until smooth. - Meat - Brown the lamb on high heat in a dutch oven. Add the blended mix followed by vegetable stock, oregano, cinnamon, and bay leaves. Cover and simmer low-and-slow for at least 7 hrs. - Serve - Dip corn tortillas in the consommé and fry on both sides. Add shredded meat and cheese and fold tortilla. Continue to fry until cheese is melted. Serve with a side of consommé for dipping. ¡Buen provecho, User Sub! EDIT - forgot the meat in the ingredients list. Looks like a 2 or 3 lb lamb shank?
item.dc:creator: AkitaFuriosa

item.title: Freshly Stolen Memes - A Dump for Paul #19
item.description: Dog tax of Louie. Louie belonged to Paul (from the title!) who is dying of lung cancer. He has been given 6 months. He is in dire straits financially since he cannot work and only recently got approved for Medicade. (He is 36 and has 2 kids). He has sold his possessions of value and this fundraiser is a last resort option for him. He has applied for disability and various forms of both state and local aid, but he is still waiting, and in the meantime he has to pay rent. He needs money for medical bills, rent, prescriptions, food, and eventually end of life expenses. If you are of a giving mind or have recently been possessed by a charitable ghost, he has recently set up a PayPal fundraiser here >> https://www.paypal.com/pools/c/8IwCPIC0xc (Previous fundraiser was here https://www.paypal.com/pools/c/8HFMSlCe6u) Please get any weird/new lumps and bumps on yourself checked, get weird coughs checked, and get a yearly physical if you can, it might save your life. Previous Dumps for Paul 1 - https://imgur.com/gallery/K0dLkFj 2- https://imgur.com/gallery/S2tHMzN 3- https://imgur.com/gallery/0vwotvp 4- https://imgur.com/gallery/63ld6W5 5 - https://imgur.com/gallery/KZM2YJk 6- https://imgur.com/gallery/pHlA2CO 7-https://imgur.com/gallery/dBm3gVd 8-https://imgur.com/gallery/43RiEnM 9-https://imgur.com/gallery/SOkxyfm 10- https://imgur.com/gallery/4IBWVn1 11-https://imgur.com/gallery/Q2WKIPi 12 - https://imgur.com/gallery/KkoK6mb 13- https://imgur.com/gallery/A3PI7ur 14- https://imgur.com/gallery/z8a2fuX 15 - https://imgur.com/gallery/59sfisF 16 - https://imgur.com/gallery/uvZueER 17 - https://imgur.com/gallery/Dmi0hX3 18 - https://imgur.com/gallery/5qbONFr

item.title: I Painted This Today
item.description: Thanks for watching! View the full process video on my YouTube channel here: https://youtu.be/3vZdj-mXRf0 This is "Towering" oil on a 16" x 20" canvas. If you’re interested, My Etsy shop: www.etsy.com/shop/HaleyGrecoArtwork My Instagram: www.instagram.com/haley.greco.artwork Most viral edit: Thank you so much you guys!! You inspire me!

item.title: Finally finished my Witcher armor!!
item.description:
I’m so stoked! It’s not screen accurate because there are no female Witchers but it is based on season 2! I’ll be adding more things to it to make it a full cosplay, but the armor is pretty final ?
Actually had to finish it up at the faire because I hadn’t had enough time to add all the buckles beforehand ?
And got to fight some orcs! If you want to see some of my other armor things I’m @thetinkertank on socials! FP edit: thank you all so much!! ☺️☺️
item.dc:creator: colorlessyoshi

item.title: Ukrainian commander, surrounded by Russians, makes impassioned plea to world leaders
item.dc:creator: ShadowWarrior6

item.title: The way corgis go up stairs
item.description: First off, sauce, but Facebook, but better quality...but Facebook....so I leave the tortuous choice to you! Geordi La Corgi: https://fb.watch/cuVAfF8QGY/ My corgo used to be a #3 all the way. But these days, DM (Degenerative Myelopathy) is making it a lot more difficult, so she has a new method called "the humans carry me like a princess!" DM is a genetic disease that CAN be bred out, as there is testing and quality breeders shouldn't breed two carriers, let alone a full positive dog. I know there's a lot of debate and emotion about adopting vs shopping, but that's honestly somewhat irrelevant. My dog who has DM was adopted....from a humane society...as a 3mo puppy - in hindsight, likely because her breeder did genetic testing and realized she was double +. I'm saying all of this to raise awareness and encourage all who choose to go through a breeder to do their research and ensure their breeder is testing their dogs, especially BEFORE breeding! "Luckily", DM is not painful, but I have zero doubt it's confusing for them - mine constantly gives her legs confused "why you no work right?!" looks - it's heartbreaking. Eventually, DM is fatal, as they'll become unable to breathe as it works up the spine. To be clear, well bred corgis can be very healthy, hearty dogs - they are a working breed who were designed to herd cattle - hence why they are short - the cows are way more likely to kick over them than to kick and injure them. It's the reason working corgis have their tails docked - so cows can't bite them. Corgis are wonderful dogs (I mean, they're all good dogs, but we're talking about corgis on this post) with feisty, spunky, clown personalities, who can do a 10mile hike and still have energy, but can also easily fit in a sedan with multiple people. They deserve better than to go out unable to run, or even walk, because of a disease that could literally be bred out within a few generations. Okay, I'll step off my soapbox now. P.s. DM does not only effect corgis - German shepherds, boxers, and some other breeds are also more prone to it. If you'd like to learn more about DM, Wikipedia gives a good rundown, as well as have some good sources: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Canine_degenerative_myelopathy?wprov=sfla1
item.dc:creator: Corgimus

item.dc:creator: oldbaldjewishman

item.title: 50 movie recommendations
item.description:
127 Hours While mountain climbing in Moab Utah, Aron Ralston falls into a canyon and becomes trapped when a large boulder pins his arm. The film mainly focuses on this moment and since he is alone throughout most of the runtime, he converses with his video diary (which is also the camera) while he describes his predicament to the audience. https://www.imdb.com/title/tt1542344/?ref_=nv_sr_srsg_0 Since the film's release, dozens of nitwits have traveled to the same spot the incident took place in and ended up needing rescue because apparently getting stuck in an isolated desert canyon is some peoples' idea of fun: https://www.cracked.com/article_20543_the-5-most-insanely-misunderstood-morals-famous-stories.html
1917 During World War 1, two soldiers cross enemy territory in a race against time to deliver a message to allied forces that will prevent the regiment from walking into a trap. https://www.imdb.com/title/tt8579674/?ref_=fn_al_tt_1 Filmed as one long unbroken camera shot, this movie excels in making you feel like you're right there with them as they cross war torn fields, travel through dark underground tunnels, all while dodging bombs and bullets like raindrops. The tension during quiet moments is just as intense as the chaotic firefights that break out with alarming regularity, it's definitely one of those edge-of-your-seat movies.
28 Days Later A deadly virus breaks out in the UK, causing devastation and massive casualties. A month later, a bicycle courier wakes up in a hospital to find the entire city seemingly deserted. https://www.imdb.com/title/tt0289043/?ref_=nv_sr_srsg_0 Hard to believe this film is already two decades old, I saw this in theaters when I was a teenager and thought it was fantastic. The 8 millimeter camera combined with the often haunting and sometimes beautiful soundtrack left an impression on me back then and it still holds up well today twenty years later.
Alone A young woman traveling to another state encounters a creepy guy and seems to run into him at every stop she makes. https://www.imdb.com/title/tt7711170/?ref_=nv_sr_srsg_7 The "young woman VS deranged lunatic" genre is certainly nothing new in cinema but I decided to give this a try anyway. It does a great job of taking a familiar trope and applying a fresh and modern spin. Nothing you haven't seen before but still manages to be entertaining and memorable.
The Autopsy of Jane Doe A father and son perform an autopsy on a unidentified woman. As they search for clues as to who she is and what she died from, they find strange and cryptic markings and symbols on the body. https://www.imdb.com/title/tt3289956/?ref_=nv_sr_srsg_0 While the title might suggest that this would be your standard gore-fest, the movie is an excellent blend of both mystery and horror. I was invested right away and watching each new development unfold in between creepy otherworldly events definitely held my attention for the whole runtime.
Baby Driver A getaway driver with tinnitus listens to custom mix tapes while assisting a crew of thieves. https://www.imdb.com/title/tt3890160/?ref_=nv_sr_srsg_0 Smart criminals, pulse-pounding stunts and a near constant soundtrack of absolute bangers make this film a ton of fun. It isn't often you see a movie from the perspective of a regular guy so it's a nice change of pace without skimping on the action.
The Ballad of Buster Scruggs This anthology film follows six stories set in the Old West which include gunfighter/singer Buster Scruggs, a bank robber looking for easy money, a pair of traveling entertainers (one of whom has no limbs), an old prospector hoping to strike it rich, a group leading a wagon train, and five people in a stagecoach. https://www.imdb.com/title/tt6412452/?ref_=nv_sr_srsg_0 After constantly seeing the "first time?" meme posted on Imgur I decided to look up the source. Every story in this movie is entertaining though somewhat inconsistent in tone. Some are funny, some are dramatic, and the last one ends the film on a somewhat ominous note, but it's a good time all around and I certainly enjoyed it.
A Bittersweet Life An underling for a powerful mob receives orders to kill a woman, but starts to have second thoughts. https://www.imdb.com/title/tt0456912/?ref_=nv_sr_srsg_0 I love Neo noir films and this South Korean entry is a great addition, opening with a fantastic soundtrack I still listen to once in a while. I found out about the movie years ago by scrolling through fight scene compilations on Youtube one afternoon. Recommended if you're in the mood for a "protagonist in a fatalist situation" story line.
Blue Ruin A homeless man living on the beach is picked up by police and warned that the man who murdered his parents is being released from prison, reigniting a violent family feud. https://www.imdb.com/title/tt2359024/?ref_=fn_al_tt_1 This is a solid revenge/thriller film. The action scenes are reality based, so no Matrix-style cartwheel flips and spins here. This adds a good bit of tension in the seconds leading up to each encounter as the protagonist is just an average Joe with no combat training.
Cold In July A father shoots and kills a home intruder one night, leading to an encounter by a man claiming to be the burglar's father. https://www.imdb.com/title/tt1179031/?ref_=nv_sr_srsg_0 Though he will always be Dexter Morgan to me, Michael Hall is still convincing in his role as a dad just trying to protect his family from various unsavory characters. The movie seems to take place in the 80s and the soundtrack matches it as well. A weird choice but it works, give this one a watch if only to see our favorite Florida serial killer on screen again.
Coraline After moving into a new home, a young girl discovers a hidden door leading to a parallel world. https://www.imdb.com/title/tt0327597/?ref_=nv_sr_srsg_0 I hadn't watched this until a decade after it came out and i'm wondering what took me so long. After finally committing to watching it one afternoon, I thought it was the best and also weirdest animated movie i've seen. If the book is half as creepy as the movie maybe i'm grateful not to have read it after all.
Crawl A woman in Florida becomes trapped in her father's home during a category 5 hurricane. https://www.imdb.com/title/tt8364368/?ref_=nv_sr_srsg_0 While not a very complex plot, this movie gets down to business quickly and provides some good nail-biting tension. Excellent choice if you're not in the mood to be bogged down with an exposition-heavy story and want to jump right into some action.
The Crazies A toxin poisons a small town's water supply, turning the population into violent psychotics. https://www.imdb.com/title/tt0455407/?ref_=nv_sr_srsg_0 In an era where every horror film gets half a dozen sequels and a reboot/re-imagining (like the Wrong Turn franchise), i'm puzzled that this one never did. The characters are smart and react the way most of us would if we were in their shoes. A great and underrated survival horror movie.
Dawn of The Dead A remake of the 1979 film of the same name (and also a good movie), a band of survivors take shelter in a shopping mall when a zombie virus erupts throughout the world. https://www.imdb.com/title/tt0363547/?ref_=nv_sr_srsg_0 I was initially not on board with the idea of fast zombies (two words that seem to be an oxymoron to me) but when I saw this in theaters I changed my mind. Plenty of zombie-slaying goodness and frenetic action scenes with one of the most "oh shit" openings to a movie in recent memory.
District B13 A cop and former thug team up to stop a neutron bomb from destroying a city. https://www.imdb.com/title/tt0414852/?ref_=nv_sr_srsg_0 A decent older action film from my collection, not a whole lot to say except for.. Parkour!
Django Unchained A bounty hunter and freed slave team up to hunt outlaws in the Old West. https://www.imdb.com/title/tt1853728/?ref_=nv_sr_srsg_0 In my opinion this is the least Tarantino-y film he's made which might be why it's my favorite. Though entertaining, his movies can come across as a little too schlocky. Django however seems to hit just the right balance of believe-ability without sacrificing any style. Not only that, but Jackson's villain in the movie was exceedingly well crafted to the point where The Vile Eye even did a video solely about him: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=6ZsUhJpmMjo
Drag Me To Hell A loan officer desperately tries to break a curse set upon her by an evicted woman. https://www.imdb.com/title/tt1127180/?ref_=nv_sr_srsg_0 Comedic horror films usually aren't my thing but I enjoyed this one all the same. The scary moments are action-heavy and never boring which makes this a fine late night popcorn movie.
The Foreigner An Asian businessman plots revenge when his daughter is killed in a terrorist attack. https://www.imdb.com/title/tt1615160/?ref_=nv_sr_srsg_0 Jackie Chan takes a wild departure from his usual goofy persona in this action thriller film and completely pulls it off. He's more of an anti-hero/vigilante in this role and while there are still some martial arts moves on display, they are more grounded in reality as opposed to his ladder-swinging type scenes in other movies.
Frailty A young boy struggles to keep his family life together when his father starts killing people after an encounter with an angel. https://www.imdb.com/title/tt0264616/?ref_=nv_sr_srsg_0 This is a quiet brooding thriller that I still think about every now and then. It proves you don't need a super high budget to make an engaging and memorable film.
Hell or High Water Two brothers become bank robbers to raise enough money to pay off a loan on the family ranch. https://www.imdb.com/title/tt2582782/?ref_=nv_sr_srsg_0 A fantastic crime movie, this is like a Western film set during modern day and even though the protagonists are technically the villains you can't help but root for them even as the long arm of the law closes in around them.
The Hills Have Eyes A family traveling through the desert encounter a group of radiation-exposed cannibals. https://www.imdb.com/title/tt0454841/?ref_=nv_sr_srsg_0 This action-horror movie is more brutal and traumatizing than the original film and is one of the better remakes made, it has a "grind house drive-in theater" feel to it.
The House An anthology film featuring three stories about a poor family of humans, a rat guy developer, and a group of cats all living in the same house across different time periods. https://www.imdb.com/title/tt11703050/?ref_=nv_sr_srsg_3 I saw this one very recently and absolutely enjoyed the heck out of it. The characters are crafted from knitted felt as opposed to the typical sculpted clay you would expect from clarinet-heavy animated movies. The first story is spooky, the second one is strange, and the third is touching. By far my favorite Netflix film to date.
Jane Got A Gun When a woman is targeted by her former outlaw gang, she seeks help from her former fiance to even the odds. https://www.imdb.com/title/tt2140037/?ref_=nv_sr_srsg_0 While not performing well at the box office, I love a good Western film and this one certainly delivers what you'd expect from one. Slower paced than most movies of the genre, characters genuinely talk to each other instead of spouting witty one-liners which is a pleasant departure from the typical formula.
Joy Ride Three friends incur the wrath of a deranged trucker after a prank ends badly. https://www.imdb.com/title/tt0206314/?ref_=nv_sr_srsg_0 A fine and memorable B-movie thriller, the villain is mostly unseen for most of the runtime so instead we hear his gravelly voice through a CB radio throughout the film. "Candy Cane.."
Kung Fu Hustle Residents of a sprawling tenement are beset by an axe-wielding gang. https://www.imdb.com/title/tt0373074/?ref_=nv_sr_srsg_0 Just as the tagline suggests, this movie has great action, funny humor, and even manages a few genuinely heartfelt moments between some of the characters. Basically an old Kung Fu movie made for a modern audience with references to other films for good measure. As with any martial arts film, watch it with subtitles and not dubbed. The action and dialogue are mostly separate so you won't miss anything reading the on screen text.
Lucky Number Slevin After being mistaken for someone else, an average Joe gets pulled into a war between two rival crime bosses. https://www.imdb.com/title/tt0425210/?ref_=nv_sr_srsg_0 A solid crime thriller with plenty of twists and turns, it also features Morgan Freeman in the rare villain role similar to another entry in this list.
The Man From Nowhere A former special agent stops at nothing to rescue a neighbor girl from drug traffickers. https://www.imdb.com/title/tt1527788/?ref_=nv_sr_srsg_0 Another South Korean entry, this flick features more strong performances and brutal action that the country has perfected in cinema.
The Mortuary Collection A young woman applying for work at a mortuary is treated to several spooky tales by the mortician. https://www.imdb.com/title/tt7781432/?ref_=nv_sr_srsg_0 This is the kind of film that would make for a cool tradition to watch with all your friends and family during October. Good for both older children and adults alike, the scares on on par with that of homes decorated for Halloween, so leaning more towards fun than downright scarring like other horror movies.
Nightcrawler A man with no conscious discovers he can make money by filming and selling footage of gruesome crime scenes. https://www.imdb.com/title/tt2872718/?ref_=nv_sr_srsg_0 Oh man, was this movie good. Gyllenhaal's character is the most accurate portrayal of a sociopath you'll ever see. He's got this constant bug-eyed expression the whole film and it's almost as though his character is an alien in a human body observing people and trying to learn how to pattern his behavior after them. Another great video on this was done by TVE: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=1wHtToZZCfs
Ninja Assassin A ninja clan hunts down one of their former members after he abandons them. https://www.imdb.com/title/tt1186367/?ref_=nv_sr_srsg_0 Light on story but heavy on stylish action, think of this movie as the fast food version of a martial arts film; not super complex but it is satisfying to watch.
Nobody A dull father becomes involved in a war with a Russian crime syndicate. https://www.imdb.com/title/tt7888964/?ref_=nv_sr_srsg_0 I have no idea what some people have against this movie but they strike me as the kind of curmudgeons who hate music, laughter, and joy. This is the king of popcorn films and pure dumb fun to watch. Gather some friends, order pizza and enjoy the spectacle on a weekend night, it's everything an action film should be: a damn good time.
No Sudden Move When a heist goes wrong, a group of criminals must work together to uncover who set them up. https://www.imdb.com/title/tt11525644/?ref_=nv_sr_srsg_0 Set in the 1950s, this crime drama is an excellent throwback to the days before modern technology dominated our daily lives. Who doesn't enjoy a good heist film?
O Brother, Where Art Thou? In the 1930s, three convicts escape a chain gang to find a hidden treasure stash. https://www.imdb.com/title/tt0190590/?ref_=nv_sr_srsg_0 I actually didn't like this movie the first time I watched it as I just didn't "get it" so to speak. It was one of those films I had to view a couple of times before it grew on me enough to enjoy it. Funny and endlessly quotable, with a great soundtrack to boot.
Old Henry A widowed farmer and his son are visited by an injured man with a satchel of money, and soon after a gang of lawmen come searching for him. https://www.imdb.com/title/tt12731980/?ref_=nv_sr_srsg_0 I can't get enough Western movies and this one is a welcome addition to the collection. Strong performances and cinematography along with a great reveal towards the end.
The Orphanage A woman buys and reopens her former orphanage home for her adopted son, who begins to make imaginary friends. https://www.imdb.com/title/tt0464141/?ref_=nv_sr_srsg_0 This is an absolutely fantastic film, more like a drama with scary elements than an outright horror movie. The ending garnered solo applause from me when I was finished watching it.
Pan's Labyrinth A young girl and her mother are taken in by the captain of a Spanish army, where she is visited by a mysterious faun with tasks for her to complete. https://www.imdb.com/title/tt0457430/?ref_=nv_sr_srsg_0 Another dark fantasy film reminiscent of a fairy tale tailored for adults instead of children including a brutally evil villain with yet another The Vile Eye video: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=t1yYBsdC0jU
Red Hill Taking place over the course of one day, the ultimate badass escapes prison and gives a young cop a difficult first day on the job. https://www.imdb.com/title/tt1530983/?ref_=nv_sr_srsg_0 Another Western film set during modern day. Plot armor can get a little ridiculous at times but it doesn't detract from the overall theme and style of the movie.
The Revenant Left for dead after being savaged by a bear, a frontiersman fights to stay alive as he makes his way back to camp. https://www.imdb.com/title/tt1663202/?ref_=nv_sr_srsg_0 Jokes about Leo aside, this is still a visually striking movie that makes you feel like you're part of the vast freezing wilderness in which it takes place. I felt the need to bundle up for cold weather just to watch it.
The Road A father desperately tries to protect his son from the perils of a post-apocalyptic event. https://www.imdb.com/title/tt0898367/?ref_=nv_sr_srsg_0 The least fun entry in this list but through the grey bleakness lies a great adaptation of the McCarthy novel. The soundtrack is equally haunting and full of sonder.
Shoot 'Em Up A gun-toting vigilante defends a newborn child from hired goons who want him dead. https://www.imdb.com/title/tt0465602/?ref_=nv_sr_srsg_0 Like a video game movie based off a video game that wasn't created, this action-packed feature has plenty of one-liners, over the top fight scenes and infinity-hundred double fisted gunshots. Tons of fun to watch.
Sin City An anthology film where villains and anti-heroes collide in the gloomy town of Sin City. https://www.imdb.com/title/tt0401792/?ref_=nv_sr_srsg_0 Part of the reason this movie was so good was because of the choice to copy the graphic novel almost exactly from the written dialogue to camera angles. The only differences are minuscule and don't affect the story in any significant way. If only video game films would do the same we wouldn't have so many terrible adaptations out there.
There Will Be Blood A mineral prospector becomes an oil baron after receiving a tip on a major deposit. https://www.imdb.com/title/tt0469494/?ref_=nv_sr_srsg_0 There are arguably no bad DDL films out there and this proves to be no exception. As his fortune grows, his clothing gets nicer and his moral fiber begins to unravel, giving audiences a glimpse at how wealth can transform someone into a hateful and brooding man. Still another entry for the Analyzing Evil channel: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dC0m7bXX6Yg
Tokyo! - 2008 This anthology film follows the stories of a young woman struggling to find her sense of self-worth, a strange man emerging from the sewers to cause havoc, and a shut-in who receives a visitor after a series of earthquakes. https://www.imdb.com/title/tt0976060/?ref_=nv_sr_srsg_0 I don't quite remember how I found this movie but i'm glad that I did, it's always nice to see the kind of creativity people can come up with during a limited runtime.
Trick 'r Treat Five stories take place during Halloween night in this anthology film. https://www.imdb.com/title/tt0862856/plotsummary?ref_=tt_ov_pl By far the best classic Halloween-themed movie since The Nightmare Before Christmas with a fine balance of comedy and horror. If you love Halloween you gotta make film this part of your October holiday tradition.
Triangle A group of friends on a yacht find an abandoned ship deserted in the ocean. https://www.imdb.com/title/tt1187064/?ref_=nv_sr_srsg_0 More trippy than scary, it's tough to describe the film without spoiling it so if you haven't seen it then it's best to watch the movie without knowing anything about it before you do. Almost reminiscent of a Twilight Zone episode.
Undisputed 2 In this sequel unrelated to the first film, a professional boxing champion is framed for a drug crime and sent to a Russian prison. https://www.imdb.com/title/tt0443676/?ref_=nv_sr_srsg_0 I was working at Hollywood Video as my second job ages ago. One day a guy comes in all in a lather and asks if we have Undisputed 2. I'd never heard of it but after checking the computer it showed as being checked out but due back in a couple days. The same guy would stop in every day that week to ask if it had been returned yet, he gushed so much about the movie that when I finally saw it returned one morning I grabbed the DVD and hid it under the counter specifically so I could rent it to him when he showed up again. Finally I got it to him and when he brought it back I rented it for myself to see what all the fuss was about. This is a solid martial arts action movie with a focus on less-unrealistic MMA fight scenes, the villain became iconic and ended up becoming the protagonist in the next couple of sequels.
Unleashed A crime lord trains a man to become a vicious enforcer to collect debts and earn money in underground fight clubs. https://www.imdb.com/title/tt0342258/?ref_=nv_sr_srsg_3 One of the better Jet Li films, this one has more heartfelt dialogue between characters than what you would expect from an action movie.
Wanted An office worker who experiences panic attacks is recruited by a group of assassins. https://www.imdb.com/title/tt0493464/?ref_=nv_sr_srsg_2 I can't get enough of those action-packed popcorn films and this is another great entry for that category, saw it in theaters and it still holds up to this day.
Wolfwalkers A young huntress discovers a wild tribal girl who can control a pack of wolves through magic. https://www.imdb.com/title/tt5198068/?ref_=nv_sr_srsg_0 Decided to watch this on a whim one afternoon and was absolutely delighted by this whimsical story. Sort of like a cross between Brave and How To Train Your Dragon, this beautifully animated movie is great for the whole family and for anyone who loves the fantasy genre.
The Wrestler An aging pro wrestler struggles to maintain the hobby he loves while trying to reconnect with his estranged daughter. https://www.imdb.com/title/tt1125849/?ref_=nv_sr_srsg_0 One of the best for last, I saw this movie on my birthday in a run-down theater deep in SLC which was the only place showing it at the time. This somewhat enhanced the experience and paralleled the story happening on screen as well. I recommend this film to people who do not watch pro wrestling because not only is it a great movie but also because it's a very real representation of what happens in the real world; people with nagging injuries struggling to make a fraction of their previous salary as they put everything on the line to entertain the fans. And that wraps up my list of movies I have personally seen and recommend you watch. If you have any other films similar in theme to the ones i've listed here, feel free to share them in the comment section below.
item.dc:creator: JebidiahBeetus

item.title: Two-hour old Imgur accounts be like....
item.dc:creator: Mech0T1

item.title: Absolute Thievery
item.description: Hope you got a chuckle
item.dc:creator: pinstripe25
